response:
To find the **characteristic polynomial** of a matrix $ A $, we compute the determinant of $ A - \lambda I $, where $ I $ is the identity matrix of the same size as $ A $, and then form the polynomial $ \det(A - \lambda I) $.

Given matrix:

$$
A = \begin{pmatrix}
-4 & -13 & 10 \\
-9 & 10 & 4 \\
12 & -5 & -14
\end{pmatrix}
$$

We compute $ A - \lambda I $:

$$
A - \lambda I = \begin{pmatrix}
-4 - \lambda & -13 & 10 \\
-9 & 10 - \lambda & 4 \\
12 & -5 & -14 - \lambda
\end{pmatrix}
$$

Now compute the determinant:

$$
\det(A - \lambda I) = \begin{vmatrix}
-4 - \lambda & -13 & 10 \\
-9 & 10 - \lambda & 4 \\
12 & -5 & -14 - \lambda
\end{vmatrix}
$$

We will expand this determinant using cofactor expansion or a calculator.

---

### Step-by-step expansion:

Let’s denote the matrix as:

$$
\begin{pmatrix}
a & b & c \\
d & e & f \\
g & h & i
\end{pmatrix}
$$

Then:

$$
\det = a(ei - fh) - b(di - fg) + c(dh - eg)
$$

Apply this to our matrix:

$$
a = -4 - \lambda, \quad b = -13, \quad c = 10 \\
d = -9, \quad e = 10 - \lambda, \quad f = 4 \\
g = 12, \quad h = -5, \quad i = -14 - \lambda
$$

Now compute:

$$
\det = (-4 - \lambda)[(10 - \lambda)(-14 - \lambda) - (4)(-5)] - (-13)[(-9)(-14 - \lambda) - (4)(12)] + 10[(-9)(-5) - (10 - \lambda)(12)]
$$

Let’s compute each part step
